question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user with no credentials call GET /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/crypto for any arbitrary account ID, enumerate all spenders who have been granted crypto spending authority over that account, and use that information to front-run or impersonate authorized spenders in downstream transactions without the account owner's knowledge or approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/allowances/tokens for any victim account and fully enumerate all token spending allowances granted to third-party spenders, exposing the complete authorization state of the account and enabling targeted exploitation of those delegated permissions without any owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any authentication or authorization middleware on the router defined in accountRoute.js to read crypto allowances for accounts they do not own, thereby learning which spenders are pre-authorized to move funds, and use that knowledge to craft transactions that abuse those allowances without the owner's consent?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractCryptoAllowancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted spender.id filter with multiple eq operators to the /allowances/crypto endpoint, causing the spenderInValues array to accumulate all spender IDs into a single IN clause, and thereby bypass any intended per-spender query restriction to retrieve the full set of authorized spenders for a victim account without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the static ownerCondition (`${CryptoAllowance.OWNER} = $1`) which is always set to the path-supplied accountId with no caller identity check, to read the complete list of authorized spenders for any account on the network, enabling unauthorized knowledge of who can transact on behalf of that account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user call the /allowances/tokens endpoint with a victim's EVM address as the idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter, resolve it to an internal account ID via EntityService.getEncodedId(), and retrieve all token spending allowances granted by that account without any signature or ownership verification?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getNftsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/nfts with a spender.id filter to enumerate all NFTs for which a specific spender has been granted approval on a victim account, revealing the full scope of delegated NFT transfer authority without the owner's knowledge or approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both spender.id eq filters (populating spenderIdInFilters) and range filters (populating spenderIdFilters) simultaneously to the /nfts endpoint, causing the multi-union query to return NFT allowance data across a broader spender range than intended, exposing unauthorized authorization state of a victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the amountCondition filter (`${CryptoAllowance.AMOUNT} > 0`) to confirm that a specific spender still holds an active (non-zero) allowance on a victim account, and use this as a real-time oracle to determine when to execute unauthorized transactions using that spender's delegated authority?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user paginate through all pages of the /allowances/crypto response using the next link generated by getPaginationLink(), systematically enumerating every spender authorized to transact on behalf of a victim account, with no rate limiting or authentication enforced by the route definition in accountRoute.js?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountTokenAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user use the spender.id and token.id range filters in extractTokenMultiUnionQuery() to perform a binary search across all token allowances of a victim account, efficiently mapping the complete authorization graph of which spenders can transfer which tokens without the owner's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a specially crafted idOrAliasOrEvmAddress path parameter (e.g., an EVM address that resolves to a system account like the treasury at shard.realm.2) to the allowances/crypto or allowances/tokens endpoints, and retrieve the full authorization state of privileged system accounts without any access control enforced by the route?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id with a gt or lt range operator to the /allowances/crypto endpoint, causing extractCryptoAllowancesQuery() to build a range condition that returns all allowances for spenders in a numeric range, enabling bulk enumeration of authorized spenders across many accounts without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user call GET /:idOrAliasOrEvmAddress/rewards for any account and retrieve staking reward transfer history, which reveals the account's staking authorization state (staked_node_id, staked_account_id) without any ownership verification, enabling inference of delegation relationships that could be exploited to authorize transactions without owner approval?
